nodes.label: AI as drafting assistant for professional correspondence
nodes.grounding_span: I primarily use AI to draft up emails and correspondence with my work's customers

nodes.label: AI use under time pressure
nodes.grounding_span: When working on a time crunch, I generally handle those tasks by myself

nodes.label: AI use for impersonal communications
nodes.grounding_span: If I have a personal relationship with a company, I like to handle those type of tasks myself

nodes.label: AI refinement iteration
nodes.label_negative: Manual override after AI fails to meet expectations
nodes.grounding_span: I actually normally have AI refine things once or twice, and then if AI isn't getting what I want correct, I'll go in and make changes manually

nodes.label: AI adoption in small company
nodes.grounding_span: I'm the only one who uses AI in my workflow. I think there's a fear of AI with the other employees

nodes.label: AI for high-stakes communication
nodes.label_negative: Risk of AI misstep in sensitive contexts
nodes.grounding_span: It gave me the perfect blueprint on the perfect email for this person

nodes.label: AI skill development
nodes.grounding_span: I would definitely like to continue growing my skills in how I use AI in my work

nodes.label: AI for task delegation beyond writing
nodes.label_negative: Clunky AI outputs for complex tasks
nodes.grounding_span: Learning to delegate tasks to AI will be the next big thing for me- like having it create spreadsheets

nodes.label: Professional communication quality
nodes.grounding_span: It's really important for us to say the right thing to them and word things in a specific way

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: If I have more time, I like to have AI do them for me

nodes.label: Personal relationship preservation
nodes.grounding_span: It's nice to have that human, personal touch in our correspondences

nodes.label: Control over output quality
nodes.grounding_span: If AI isn't getting what I want correct, I'll go in and make changes manually

nodes.label: Workplace harmony and adoption
nodes.grounding_span: I think there's a fear of AI with the other employees

nodes.label: Successful problem resolution
nodes.grounding_span: It helped us get out of a sticky situation

nodes.label: Continuous learning and adaptation
nodes.grounding_span: I would love to continue learning AI skills to help streamline my work

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for drafting
nodes.grounding_span: It gave me the perfect blueprint on the perfect email for this person

nodes.label: Preference for manual handling under time pressure
nodes.grounding_span: When working on a time crunch, I generally handle those tasks by myself

nodes.label: Frustration with AI refinement limits
nodes.grounding_span: If AI isn't getting what I want correct, I'll go in and make changes manually

nodes.label: Understanding of colleagues' fear of AI
nodes.grounding_span: I think there's a fear of AI with the other employees. Which I totally understand!

nodes.label: Optimism about expanding AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I would love to continue learning AI skills to help streamline my work

nodes.label: Iterative refinement with manual override
nodes.grounding_span: I actually normally have AI refine things once or twice, and then if AI isn't getting what I want correct, I'll go in and make changes manually
